Title: ERF Transcription Factor OsBIERF3 Positively Contributes to Immunity against Fungal and Bacterial Diseases but Negatively Regulates Cold Tolerance in Rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsBIERF3-OE plants show growth retardation at the seedling stage, which recovers at the adult stage, while OsBIERF3-Ri plants grow similarly to wild type. Manipulation of OsBIERF3 does not negatively affect major agronomic traits and can even improve some traits. OsBIERF3-mediated cold response requires functional ABA and ethylene signaling, as ACC or ABA application alleviates, whereas AgNO3 exacerbates, the cold sensitivity of OsBIERF3-OE plants and reduces the enhanced cold tolerance of OsBIERF3-Ri plants.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsBIERF3 is an ERF transcription factor from rice that acts as a transcriptional activator, with its C-terminal region required for transactivation activity in yeast.
2. OsBIERF3 expression is induced by Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae infection, defense-related hormones (ACC, SA, MeJA, ABA), and abiotic stresses (drought, salt, cold), with stronger induction during incompatible X. oryzae pv. oryzae interactions and under cold stress.
3. Overexpression of OsBIERF3 in rice enhances resistance to Magnaporthe oryzae and Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae, whereas RNAi-mediated suppression reduces resistance, indicating that OsBIERF3 is a positive regulator of rice immunity.
4. Transcriptome and qRT-PCR analyses show that OsBIERF3 up-regulates numerous defense-related genes, including PR genes and MAPK cascade components (OsMPK3, OsMPK6, OsMEK3), and cell wall biosynthetic genes; OsBIERF3-OE plants have thicker cell walls and OsBIERF3-Ri plants thinner walls, implicating OsBIERF3 in cell wall biosynthesis.
5. OsBIERF3 negatively regulates cold tolerance: OsBIERF3-OE plants are more sensitive and OsBIERF3-Ri plants more tolerant to cold, accompanied by reduced proline accumulation and down-regulation of cold-responsive genes in OE plants and the opposite trends in Ri plants, while drought and salt tolerance are unaffected.
6. ABA and ethylene signaling are required for OsBIERF3-mediated cold response, as ACC and ABA treatments rescue the cold-sensitive phenotype of OsBIERF3-OE plants, and AgNO3 treatment diminishes the enhanced cold tolerance of OsBIERF3-Ri plants.
7. Manipulating OsBIERF3 expression does not impair major agronomic traits and may improve some yield-related traits, suggesting its potential utility for breeding disease-resistant rice in environments where cold stress is not limiting.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray-based transcriptome profiling using the Affymetrix Rice Genome Array (51,279 transcripts) was performed on leaves of 3-week-old OsBIERF3-OE vs. WT plants, identifying 3637 differentially expressed genes (2149 up-regulated, 1488 down-regulated; ≥2-fold, p < 0.05).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsBIERF3 is a transcriptional activator that positively regulates resistance to Magnaporthe oryzae and Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae and negatively regulates cold tolerance, while having no detectable effect on drought and salt tolerance; it also enhances cell wall thickness in rice.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: OsBIERF3 functions as a transcriptional activator whose C-terminal region is required for transactivation in yeast. Overexpression of OsBIERF3 up-regulates numerous defense-related genes, including PR proteins and MAPK cascade components, and cell wall biosynthetic genes, while its suppression down-regulates these genes. OsBIERF3 also modulates the expression of cold-responsive genes and alters proline accumulation under cold stress, likely by activating upstream negative regulators of cold response. Its function in cold tolerance depends on intact ABA and ethylene signaling, as ACC and ABA pretreatments restore, and AgNO3 pretreatment diminishes, cold tolerance phenotypes associated with altered OsBIERF3 expression.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: OsBIERF3 participates in rice immune signaling by transcriptionally activating components of MAPK cascades (OsMPK3, OsMPK6, OsMEK3) and defense-related genes, and contributes to cell wall biosynthesis pathways by up-regulating cellulose synthase and glucan endo-1,3-beta-glucosidase genes. In abiotic stress response, OsBIERF3 functions within ABA- and ethylene-dependent cold stress signaling pathways, where its activity leads to repression of cold-responsive genes and reduced cold tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cell wall biosynthesis, including cellulose and β-1,3-glucan synthesis.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: OsBIERF3 -->(activation)--> OsMPK3; OsBIERF3 -->(activation)--> OsMPK6; OsBIERF3 -->(activation)--> OsMEK3; OsBIERF3 -->(activation)--> OsCesA7; OsBIERF3 -->(activation)--> OsCesA9; OsBIERF3 -->(activation)--> LOC_Os03g12140 (glucan endo-1,3-beta-glucosidase); OsBIERF3 -->(activation)--> LOC_Os10g20650 (glucan endo-1,3-beta-glucosidase); OsBIERF3 --| (inhibition)--> OsMyb; OsBIERF3 --| (inhibition)--> OsCDPK7; OsBIERF3 --| (inhibition)--> OsFer1; OsBIERF3 --| (inhibition)--> OsLti6a; OsBIERF3 --| (inhibition)--> OsLti6b; OsBIERF3 --| (inhibition)--> OsTrx23.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ERF (AP2/ERF) transcription factor, member of group IX of the rice ERF subfamily, containing an AP2 DNA-binding domain.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsBIERF3 transcript levels are induced by Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae infection, with stronger and earlier induction in the incompatible BB10–PXO86 interaction than in the compatible IR24–PXO86 interaction. OsBIERF3 expression is also induced by the defense-related hormones ACC (ethylene precursor), salicylic acid, methyl jasmonate and abscisic acid, and by abiotic stresses including rapid dehydration, high salinity (150 mM NaCl) and cold (4°C), with cold treatment causing up to ~11-fold induction at 24 h.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Biotic treatments: inoculation with Magnaporthe oryzae race ZE3 strain 97-220 and Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae strain PXO86. Hormone treatments: foliar spraying with ACC (ethylene precursor), salicylic acid, methyl jasmonate and abscisic acid. Abiotic treatments: drought (withholding water or leaf dehydration), salt stress (150 mM NaCl), and cold stress at 4°C, including cold assays combined with ACC, ABA or AgNO3 (ethylene action inhibitor) pretreatments.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Disease resistance (blast and bacterial leaf blight), abiotic stress response (cold tolerance), transcriptional regulation, and cell wall biosynthesis in rice.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Biotic stress: fungal blast disease resistance (Magnaporthe oryzae) and bacterial leaf blight resistance (Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae); Abiotic stress: cold stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Upon M. oryzae infection, OsBIERF3-OE plants show 69–78% reductions in lesion numbers and ~92–93% reductions in fungal biomass (Mo28S/OsEF1 ratios) compared with WT, whereas OsBIERF3-Ri plants show 89–204% increases in lesion numbers and ~98–387% increases in fungal biomass. After X. oryzae pv. oryzae infection, lesion lengths in OsBIERF3-OE lines are reduced by 58–64% (to ~2.0–2.3 cm) and in OsBIERF3-Ri lines increased by 40–131% (to 7.7–12.7 cm) relative to WT (~5.5 cm), and bacterial titers in OsBIERF3-OE leaves are 3.6–29.2-fold lower while those in OsBIERF3-Ri leaves are 3.2–7.7-fold higher than WT. In microarray data, OsMPK3, OsMPK6, and OsMEK3 transcripts are increased 4.12-, 5.87-, and 6.28-fold, respectively, in OsBIERF3-OE plants. Cell wall thickness in sheath tissues increases by ~1.1-fold in OsBIERF3-OE plants and decreases by 21–24% in OsBIERF3-Ri plants relative to WT. OsBIERF3 expression is induced up to 3.8–5.8-fold (BB10) vs. 1.3–2.1-fold (IR24) after X. oryzae pv. oryzae inoculation, and up to 2.45-, 4.35-, 2.52-, and 3.42-fold by ACC, SA, MeJA, and ABA, respectively; it is induced ~3.12-fold by dehydration, ~4.55-fold by NaCl at 1 h, and ~11.42-fold by cold at 24 h. Under cold stress, OsBIERF3-OE plants show survival rates <20%, OsBIERF3-Ri plants >78%, and WT ~40%; proline content decreases by >61% in OsBIERF3-OE plants and increases by >23% in OsBIERF3-Ri plants relative to WT.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: OsBIERF3-OE plants exhibit seedling growth retardation that recovers at the adult stage, whereas OsBIERF3-Ri plants grow similarly to WT. Cell walls in sheath tissues are significantly thicker in OsBIERF3-OE plants and thinner in OsBIERF3-Ri plants than in WT. Under cold stress, OsBIERF3-OE plants accumulate less free proline, whereas OsBIERF3-Ri plants accumulate more proline than WT. OsBIERF3 manipulation does not alter drought and salt tolerance or major agronomic traits such as grain yield, panicle weight, and grain number.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transgenic rice plants (cv. Xiushui 11) overexpressing OsBIERF3 (OsBIERF3-OE) or carrying an RNAi construct targeting OsBIERF3 (OsBIERF3-Ri); corresponding wild-type Xiushui 11 plants; rice cv. Yuanfengzao for hormone and abiotic stress expression analyses; near-isogenic lines IR24 and BB10 for incompatible and compatible interactions with Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Xiushui 11 (background of OsBIERF3-OE and OsBIERF3-Ri transgenic lines); Yuanfengzao; IR24; BB10
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Agrobacterium-mediated transformation to generate OsBIERF3 overexpression and RNAi lines; selection of single-copy, homozygous lines by hygromycin segregation and Southern blotting; qRT-PCR for gene expression; Affymetrix Rice Genome Array microarray for transcriptome profiling; yeast transcriptional activation assay with GAL4 DNA-binding domain fusions; pathogen inoculation assays with Magnaporthe oryzae (spray inoculation of seedlings) and Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae (leaf-clipping of adult plants); drought, salt and cold stress tolerance assays; hormone treatments with ACC, SA, MeJA and ABA; proline content measurement; TEM to measure cell wall thickness.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression and RNAi-mediated suppression of OsBIERF3 in rice (cv. Xiushui 11) generated OsBIERF3-OE and OsBIERF3-Ri lines; disease resistance was assayed by M. oryzae spray inoculation and X. oryzae pv. oryzae leaf-clipping inoculation; cold tolerance was evaluated in growth chambers at 4°C with or without pretreatment by ACC, ABA or AgNO3; drought and salt tolerance assays were performed; defense-, MAPK cascade-, cell wall- and cold-responsive gene expression was analyzed by qRT-PCR and microarray; cell wall thickness was examined by TEM; transcriptional activation activity was tested in yeast using GAL4 DNA-binding domain fusions.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: OsBIERF3 was previously identified as a rice ERF transcription factor responsive to pathogen and chemical inducer treatments; in this study its 912 bp coding sequence was cloned under the maize Ubi promoter in pCoUm for overexpression and a 400 bp 5′ fragment was used to construct a hairpin RNAi vector, and both constructs were introduced into rice cv. Xiushui 11 via Agrobacterium-mediated transformation for functional analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of OsBIERF3 in rice enhances resistance to the major diseases blast (Magnaporthe oryzae) and bacterial leaf blight (Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae) without detectable penalty on key agronomic traits (grain yield, panicle weight, grain number) or on drought and salt tolerance, although it reduces cold tolerance. The authors propose OsBIERF3 as a promising target to develop disease-resistant rice varieties for regions where cold stress is not a major constraint.
